text.title: Post-Handover Warranty Runbook Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every warranty runbook must satisfy.

rule.statement: Every step MUST start with an observable precondition (alert, ticket, signal, state). Missing precondition means skip the step, never partially execute.

rationale.source: Google SRE Workbook + aviation checklist design

rule.rationale: Steps run mid-shift fail when system state is wrong; preconditions are cheap guards that prevent secondary incidents.

rule.statement: Every action MUST name actor as role+system and the artefact produced; 'notify stakeholders' is rejected, 'on-call posts in #incident with template X' is accepted.
rule.rationale: Vague actors fail under stress; named role + system makes ownership unambiguous.

rule.statement: Every side-effect step MUST carry rollback or hard STOP + escalate. No silent failure paths.
rule.rationale: Steps without rollback strand the operator mid-procedure when judgment is worst.

rule.statement: Each phase MUST publish wall-clock budget; exceeding budget triggers escalation, not 'try harder'.
rule.rationale: Without timeboxes one misfire consumes the whole response window.

rule.statement: After execution, a structured run-record (start_ts, end_ts, branches, exceptions, artefacts) MUST be committed to the postmortem feeder.
